title: After serving prison time, Andrea James became a leading criminal justice advocate. Now she’s running for governor.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Andrea James, a criminal justice advocate and former inmate, is running for governor of Massachusetts as an independent.
James was a real estate attorney disbarred in 2010 for federal wire fraud and has since become an advocate for incarcerated women.
Running on a populist platform, James criticizes party politics for ignoring voters' needs and focuses on investment in public welfare.

title: ‘The sun will rise, the sun will set, and I’ll have lunch’: The timeless wisdom of Lou Gorman
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Lou Gorman, former GM of the Boston Red Sox, famously used humor during tense contract talks with Roger Clemens.
Clemens walked out of 1987 spring training over contract disputes, but Gorman maintained patience and confidence.
Gorman's tenure included assembling the 1986 World Series team, despite some controversial trades like Jeff Bagwell for Larry Andersen.

title: She’s running the Boston Marathon for her former patient with Morquio syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Erin Donnelly, a physical therapist, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her former patient Gio.
Gio Thompson, who had Morquio syndrome, was Donnelly's patient at Franciscan Children's and inspired her with his resilience.
Gio passed away at age 5; Donnelly runs to carry on his mantra, 'I can do hard things,' in his memory.

title: Bruins fall to Devils 4-3 in OT as US Olympic hero Jack Hughes has 3 assists
author: The Associated Press
bullets: The Boston Bruins lost 4-3 in overtime to the New Jersey Devils, affecting their playoff position in the Eastern Conference.
Jack Hughes contributed three assists for the Devils, reaching 402 career points in 414 regular-season games.
Bruins' David Pastrnak scored twice as the team blew a two-goal lead and failed to secure back-to-back victories.

title: Tyngsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Shawn Sullivan, 40, of Tyngsborough, charged with murder after remains found under his garage, possibly missing Lowell woman.
Police believe remains may belong to Jill T. Kloppenburg, missing since January 2025; connection reported by Sullivan's friend.
Investigators found patched area in Sullivan's garage using radar; Medical Examiner to identify remains and determine cause of death.

title: Why a Texas mom is running the Boston Marathon for her son — and his MGH doctor
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Adrienne Felterman, 45, from Houston, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital.
Felterman's son, Charles, has Dup15q syndrome, leading to epilepsy and developmental delays, treated by Dr. Ronald Thibert at MGH.
Funds raised by Felterman will honor her son and Dr. Thibert, highlighting MGH's impact on their lives.

title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Ben Flowers from Belmont is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of his wife, Coney Flowers.
Coney was diagnosed with Acute Lymphoblastic leukemia in 2024 and is now almost two years cancer free.
Flowers is raising money for the Caring for a Cure team that supported them during Coney's treatment.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kathleen Labrie will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Massachusetts General Hospital Pediatric Cancer team.
Labrie uses her participation to honor children affected by cancer, adopting the motto 'I Run For Them.'
Donors can contribute in honor of loved ones, as Labrie shares poignant stories behind every honored individual.

title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan's documentary, 'Noah Kahan: Out of Body,' debuts on Netflix on April 13.
The film covers Kahan's personal and creative journey over a year and a half of filming.
Kahan's fourth album, 'The Great Divide,' releases on April 24, shortly after the documentary.

title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Jon Keller returns to WBZ-TV as a 'special contributor,' after being laid off last fall.
Keller's 'Keller At Large' interview show resumes Sunday, featuring Gov. Maura Healey.
Keller maintains his weekly MASSterList column and contributes to Boston magazine.
